items.title: All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026
items.summary: Digital Extremes is revealing its upcoming plans for Warframe and Soulframe at TennoCon 2026
items.content:
By Simone De Rochefort & Tomas Franzese Published Jul 11, 2026, 1:01 PM EDT Gaming Listen up, Tenno and Envoys

It's time for you to play Warframe Total War: Warhammer 40,000's amazing gameplay reveal will make you wonder if your PC can handle it After 3 years, Pokémon's biggest competition is officially out with a new release All the new Warframe and Soulframe announcements at TennoCon 2026 Image: Digital Extremes Sign in to your Polygon.com account Every year, hundreds of Warframe and Soulframe players gather in London, Ontario, the home of game developer Digital Extremes, to learn what's new in the games they love. At last year's TennoCon , Digital Extremes revealed that the game's next expansion would take players to Tau, a new solar system. It also announced three new Protoframes , and opened the doors of Soulframe to all new players.

This year, we're getting even more announcements about Digital Extremes' future plans for Warframe and Soulframe, both onstage at TennoCon and in a Twitch stream you can watch from home. As the schedule highlights, a Soulframe Devstream has just taken place, while Warframe fans shouldn't miss the 4:30 pm EDT TennoLive 2026 showcase.

To help you keep track of all the announcements and reveals, we're rounding up everything Digital Extremes announces at TennoCon this year. Keep an eye on this article during TennoCon to see what has been revealed.

As in previous years, Warframe players can enter a special in-game Relay orbiting Saturn to experience an in-game event synchronized with the TennoLive stream.

Digital Extremes is once again ready to welcome a new batch of players into Soulframe . If you sign up on the game's website before 11:59 PM EDT on July 12, you will get into and be able to play the Soulframe : Preludes pre-alpha playtest.

Image: Digital Extremes For those who get into Soulframe: Preludes this weekend, you can face a new boss called the Mendicant Reinbreaker. If you beat the Mendicant Reinbreaker, you'll be able to befriend the horse the boss rides in the future.

Digital Extremes did confirm during the Devstream that a new Fable called Warsongs will come to Soulframe: Preludes this fall. It'll feature the Vadagar Pact, a new "corrupted player progression path" with more violent abilities.

Image: Digital Extremes We got a brief look at Ben Starr's character, an antagonist named Tempest Bayor, but won't learn more about him until the TennoLive showcase later tonight. That said, Ben Starr isn't the only Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 talent making its way into Soulframe . Jennifer English, who played Maelle in Expedition 33 , will also portray a new character, the Empress of Eldveil. English's character appeared briefly at TennoCon 2025, and English herself appeared on the Devstream panel.

Image: Digital Extremes Later this year, Soulframe: Preludes players will be able to use befriended animals as mounts. A fishing minigame will also make its way to the pre-alpha.

This post will be updated with news from TennoCon 2026.

It's every game you've ever wanted and more, and every update makes it better.

items.title: Soulframe gets a massive info drop at Tennocon Live, including new bosses, mounts and Ben Starr
items.summary: One of our most anticipated free-to-play games right now has to be Soulframe, coming from Digital Extremes, the team behind the hugely popular Warframe.
items.content:
One of our most anticipated free-to-play games right now has to be Soulframe, coming from Digital Extremes, the team behind the hugely popular Warframe. With this weekend’s 11th annual TennoCon community event going on right now, the development team behind the upcoming free-to-play fantasy action RPG revealed that players everywhere can gain permanent access to Soulframe Preludes by signing up on the official site by July 12 at 11:59 PM ET/4:59 AM BST. TennoCon attendees and viewers from home should tune into TennoLive on Warframe’s Twitch or YouTube channels at 4:30 PM ET/9:30 PM BST to see the latest gameplay demo for Soulframe.

Additional reveals included a sneak peek at the Warsongs Fable quest featuring Ben Starr (Clair Obscur: Expedition 33) as an antagonist named Tempest Bayor ahead of the full TennoLive presentation, a new fishing mini-game, the Vadagar Pact showcasing a new corrupted progression path, the Mendicant Reinbreaker boss fight, and confirmation that mounts are coming to Soulframe Preludes this year.

“Our approach to storytelling in Soulframe has focused a lot on redemption, with uplifting character arcs built around salvation and not necessarily the destruction of the human body and soul,” said Geoff Crookes, Creative Director for Soulframe. “Life has many paths toward redemption, though, and with the Warsongs Fable we’re attempting to explore a smidge of that contrast to demonstrate the necessary balance and coexistence. Growth does not truly occur without a certain level of despair and fear. These are important storytelling mechanics that are incredibly relatable to the human experience and journey in life. These themes aren’t comfortable to experience, but they’re essential to our existence as temporary inhabitants of Alca. We hope our community, and players discovering Soulframe for the first time today, find a sense of connection with that.”

In celebration of community at TennoCon, Digital Extremes once again invites all players interested in joining its growing community to create an account at Soulframe.com by July 12 at 11:59 p.m. ET to secure access to Soulframe Preludes. Claim an Envoy name and explore the dozens of weapons, Fables, characters, and Pacts Preludes currently has to offer. Players can continue to access Preludes until the game’s wider public launch. Once the open sign-up window has closed, players may still sign up, but will wait for the weekly raffle until additional access is available.

Channel corruption to carve a new kind of path for redemption with a darker progression path introduced in Soulframe’s latest Fable quest, Warsongs, coming later this Fall. Wield the power of the corrupting Ichor with the new Vadagar Pact to toss exploding boomerangs, call forth ensnaring tendrils, or summon a stampede of corrosive steeds that leave behind murky pools in their wake.

Unveil the face of one of Soulframe’s main rivals to the player, Tempest Bayor, brought to life by the performance of Ben Starr. As a rare red-armed Envoy, he aligns himself with the invading Ode forces with the support of his faithful raven companion. His motivations are unknown, but the Empress of Eldveil, portrayed by Jennifer English (Clair Obscur: Expedition 33) in her first in-game appearance, seems to know more…

Break a bucking rampage of the Ode forces’ latest boss encounter, the Mendicant Reinbreaker, now available in Soulframe Preludes. Defeat the mounted menace and free his indentured steed for the potential chance to befriend it in the future.

Recall forgotten childhood pastimes of lakeside fishing with Grandfather Doyen in a new fishing gathering activity woven directly into the game’s narrative while expanding the Alca’s worldbuilding.

The wait to traverse through Alca with a full-grown wolf companion is nearing its end. Soulframe Preludes players will soon be able to test out the first highly anticipated mount system later this year!

There’s still no sign of an official release date for Soulframe, but with the permanent access you can currently get for Soulfrrame Preludes, there’s never been a better time to get involved, become part of the community, and to help shape the finished game.

items.title: Fantasy action-RPG Soulframe is open to all players for a limited time, with a new dark progression path, wolf mounts, and a Warsongs origin quest on the way
items.summary: Soulframe, the fantasy action-RPG from Warframe makers Digital Extremes, is getting wolf mounts, a Warsongs quest, and a new Vadagar progression path.
items.content:
Image credit: Digital Extremes / Rock Paper Shotgun News by Edwin Evans-Thirlwell News Editor Updated on July 11, 2026 Follow Soulframe Soulframe , the fantasy action-RPG from Warframe creators Digital Extremes, is getting wolf mounts, a Warsongs quest, and a new Vadagar progression path or "Pact" that transforms your "Envoy" character into the lovechild of Soulcalibur's Ivy and Tim Curry's Hexxus from Fern Gully. Unfamiliar with both of those? Think ichor-slathered bone warrior with whipsword. The Vadagar class addition is intriguing because it sees you channelling the powers of the Ode, Soulframe's space-faring, spellsong-controlled enemy faction, who are trying to harvest or corrupt all the world's resources and wildlife.

As ever, it's a little hard to frame all this for public consumption because Soulframe isn't, strictly speaking, "out" yet. It's still moving through a series of closed pre-alphas, or "preludes", but Digital Extremes have been pretty generous with codes. There's an open sign-up period from now till 12th July, to capitalise on the publicity from this year's Tennocon expo. If you miss the window, you can also buy a Founder's pack to gain entry along with some exclusive trinkets.

I was shown a little of the new Pact in action in the run-up to Tennocon. The Vadagar gets three different core stats, or Vespers, in place of the Virtues of the other pacts - Wrath, Doom, and Death. Their abilities include snaring foes with a pillar of goop, sending a conduit of goop through the floor, and summoning goop horses to trample everybody into puddles of goop. Goop: the substance with a thousand uses! There's also that slimy whipsword, the corrupted remnant of a Goodie weapon, which comes in handy for crowd control. And there's an extra-special goop horse you can ride once you've felled a new boss, the Mendicant Reinbreaker - pictured above, and live in the game right now.

Goop takedowns aside, what interests me about Vadagar is that it's encouraging you to see life from the Ode's perspective. Digital Extremes have stressed that this isn't a rigid binary good/evil thing - you can pick Vadagar without forever losing access to the other pacts. In general, it makes me want to write more about Soulframe's ecofable, which is opulent and earnest to the point of cutesy and cheesy. This is a game where you start each session slumbering beneath a heap of adorable birds and bunnies . You'll rescue additional cute critters from crates at Ode camps. It's all very Disney. Give us a quest where you have to save a bunch of botflies, Digital Extremes! Let me ride around on a massive cockroach pal.

I imagine that the new mounts for Envoys will be characterised as companion creatures, rather than subservient lifeforms. The existing game takes care to avoid the spectacle of animal suffering: when you defeat a wardog, you'll knock away the sorcerous helm enslaving it, rather than gutting the poor pupper. Soulframe is also getting a fishing minigame, but this was playfully described in a pre-Tennocon 2026 presentation as "vegan fishing", because you're actually cleansing fish mutated by Ode mischief. Genuinely, I wonder whether Digital Extremes have thought about adapting the Jain monastic practice of using a broom to harmlessly sweep smaller creatures out of one's path. It would make for a much slower loot grind, admittedly.

As for the Warsongs questline, it will probably mystify anybody who hasn't been keeping up with Soulframe's fast-evolving yarn. Without spoiling too much, it concerns the origins and parentage of your character, and introduces a new villain, a turncoat Envoy called Tempest Bayor, who is voiced by Ben Starr. Lastly, there's a witchy empress character voiced by Jennifer English of Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 fame.

The mounts are pencilled in for release later this year. There will be a number of Soulframe updates in advance of Warsong, introducing a new weather system and other supporting features.

A question I keep asking myself: who is Soulframe for? Its more ponderous, duel-oriented melee combat feel like a bid for players turned off by Warframe's extreme agility and AOE overkill, but its oblique and singsong fairytale world wouldn't look out of place in the older game's solar system. It also puts a similar emphasis on loot drops, crafting and grinding. Speaking as somebody who's barely touched Warframe since reviewing the 2013 version on Xbox One, I do prefer Soulframe's methodical combat and sleepier vein of fantasy , but it still fundamentally feels like a game for people who love Warframe - i.e. people who are prepared to make it a five thousand hour habit, and memorise all the quirks of the lore together with the arcane in-game economy.

This article is based on a trip to Tennocon 2026, with Digital Extremes paying for travel and accommodation. An earlier version of this article infringed on an embargo for coverage of certain details - it has been updated at Digital Extremes's request to remove this information. The details in question will be discussed in a future piece.

0 Heirloom Collections are among the most exclusive gear available in Warframe, designed to honor the passage of time and the dedication of the Tenno. And the legendary gunslinger, Mesa, is the honoree of our latest Heirloom Collection!

Customize Mesa Heirloom’s look to take on any bounty with her signature Signa and removable Duster attachment. The peerless duelist’s trusty Regulators receive the Heirloom treatment as well.

As with previous Heirloom Collections, there are two ways you can add Mesa Heirloom items to your Arsenal: purchase the Mesa Heirloom Collection via the in-game Market for Platinum, or choose to pay in your local currency (and receive an additional 425 Platinum to spend as you please).

items.title: Warframe Guide: All New Augment Mods & How to Get Them
items.summary: Get all 4 new Augment mods from Warframe Jade Shadows: Constellations. See every effect, mod cost, and which Syndicates sell them.
items.content:
The Jade Shadows: Constellations update brought four new Augment mods to Warframe , each tied to a specific frame and sold through particular Syndicates. These aren't minor tweaks. Depending on your build, they can meaningfully shift how a frame plays, especially for energy-hungry setups and Overguard stacking. Here's every new mod, what it actually does, and exactly where to buy it.

The update added four Augment mods covering Koumei , Temple , Dante , and Nokka . Every one of them costs 9 mod capacity at max rank, so plan your builds accordingly before slotting them in.

important All four mods cost 9 capacity when maxed. If you're running a tight build, check your available capacity before committing to one of these. How do you get each Augment mod? Every new Augment mod comes from Syndicate offerings , so you need standing with the right faction before you can buy one. Each mod is available from two Syndicates, giving you some flexibility depending on which factions you're already invested in. If you need to build standing from scratch or switch allegiances, check out the Warframe Syndicates guide for the most efficient farming routes.

tip If you're aligned with New Loka, you have access to both Kumihimo Loading and Rhythm Guard from a single faction, which makes double-dipping on standing efficient. Syndicate standing required to buy

For energy-focused builds, Noctua Swarm is the standout. The Alternate Fire mechanic releases Paragrimms that stay active for 15 full seconds, covering an 8m radius around wherever you aim. The combination of silencing enemies and funneling their energy to your team makes this genuinely strong in group content where energy economy matters. If your Dante build already leans into energy generation, this mod feeds directly into that loop.

Rhythm Guard rewards precise ability timing with up to 1600 Overguard per Beat. The scaling is real: you start at 100 and double on each successful Backbeat ability use. Miss a single Beat and the counter resets to zero, so this mod punishes sloppy play hard. It's high-ceiling, high-risk, and worth it if you've already got Temple's rhythm system down.

Kumihimo Loading adds a meaningful payoff to Koumei's weapon kill passive. After 6 kills under the passive's effect, you bank a guaranteed 6-roll die. Holding to cast an empowered Kumihimo and spending those dice creates a reliable damage spike that removes the randomness Koumei players typically have to accept. If variance frustrates you on Koumei, this mod is the direct answer.

Reroot Rampage ties directly into Nokka's Reroot orb collection, summoning extra Sprodlings that deal 250 Toxin damage with boosted Critical Chance on every hit. For players who build around Nokka's mushroom damage output, this stacks well with existing Sprodling-focused setups. It's a narrower mod than Noctua Swarm but fits cleanly into the right build.

warning All four mods are frame-specific Augments. They only function when equipped on their respective Warframe, so don't slot Noctua Swarm onto a non-Dante build expecting any effect. How do Augment mods fit into your broader build? Augment mods occupy a mod slot like any other, so the 9-capacity cost at max rank is a real consideration. For frames that are already capacity-tight, you may need a Forma or two to make room without sacrificing core mods. The Warframe modding guide covers how to optimize capacity and prioritize slots if you're not sure where to cut.

For players just getting into the Constellations content, the new frames Sirius and Orion also arrived with this update. Getting them involves a separate acquisition path, covered in the Sirius and Orion farming guide .

For everything else the update added, including the full quest prerequisites and what to expect from the Constellations content, the Warframe guides hub has the full picture.

items.title: Kuva weapon tier list - Warframe
items.content:
Guide Kuva weapon tier list - Warframe Off the Lich.

Kuva Weapons were the weapon system introduced to Warframe that had a progenitor system. Farmed from Liches, they brought some life back into older Grineer weapons that had fallen by the wayside. Some of them are still exceptionally strong, but others might not be worth your time, except as mastery fodder.

Below, you will see my rankings for the Kuva weapons as they currently stand in the game. This only looks at these weapons and compares them to each other; there is no consideration for whether the Kuva weapons are stronger or weaker than similar Coda, Tenet, or Incarnon weapons.

The way my personal tier lists always work is that I think about the specific use case for a weapon rather than wish it could do something it doesn't need to do. Some weapons are significantly more powerful when used with particular Warframes, and I try to exclude these cases from my consideration. If there is something you should really consider, I'll give it a little section below.

And there you go. I will update this as often as it makes sense, such as when I or someone else discovers a new build or interaction that makes something very powerful or when changes occur based on updates and new releases.

It should also be noted that the above list is entirely subjective, based on my own preferred playstyle and the type of content I like to play in the game. You should really be grabbing weapons and playing with them yourself, experimenting with builds, and having fun, although I understand the place these lists have in helping people make decisions when they are low on time for games with such huge volumes of content as Warframe.

You can find more helpful guides on our Warframe page .

Shacknews staff does not use generative artificial intelligence (AI) in their content. Shacknews strictly prohibits the use of its content for AI training or to generate text, including text in the style or format used for this publication. Shacknews reserves all rights to this work.

Hailing from Ireland, Aidan has been conditioned by local weather conditions to survive hours at his PC grinding through whatever game is offering the lowest possible drop rates for loot. He thinks the easiest way to figure out what fans of games want to read is to just be a fan of games. You can normally find him logged into Warframe, Destiny, or a gacha game. You can reach out to him on X @scannerbarkly .

Aidan O'Brien posted a new article, Kuva weapon tier list - Warframe

items.title: Titania Prime and Gara Prime Enter Prime Resurgence
items.summary: Dominate your enemies through diminutive fae mischief or shatter their corporeal form with Prime Resurgence! Add Titania Prime and Gara Prime, along with their signature Prime Weapons and Prime Accessories, to your Arsenal today with Regal
items.content:
Dominate your enemies through diminutive fae mischief or shatter their corporeal form with Prime Resurgence! Add Titania Prime and Gara Prime, along with their signature Prime Weapons and Prime Accessories, to your Arsenal today with Regal Aya.

Titania Prime

From a flowery bed to the clouds above, a fae queen blissfully invites her foes to tumble down again. Titania Prime deploys legions of butterflies to disorient and dominate any adversary, making her an excellent pick for those looking for a Warframe with strong crowd control capabilities. Razorwing, Titania Prime’s 4th Ability, even allows her to shrink down and take flight, becoming part of the mystifying swarm she commands.

Gara Prime

Even the faintest touch from Gara Prime is enough to shatter both mind and body of any that stand in her way. This formidable glassblower is able to reshape the battlefield through molten crystal that can splinter enemies while also bolstering defenses of allies. Gara Prime’s high survivability and aptitude for significant damage output should be appealing to anyone who has no interest in being a glass cannon.

WHAT ARE PRIMES?

When you require the finest and most powerful armaments, only Primes will do. Exquisitely gilded, every Prime Warframe, Weapon and item exists in its purest form — just as the Orokin intended. If you're new to Warframe, you can learn more about the Prime Resurgence Program by visiting our official FAQs page and watching our community video guide below:

items.title: Warframe of Mind: In praise of Warframe’s optimistic nihilism
items.content:
I had originally planned a very different column to run today, and it would have been part of Vague Patch Notes , but then Microsoft happened. It was a more serious column, and for a moment I considered writing a column about that, but I found that it just kept devolving into articulate but not terribly interesting rage. I don’t need to tell you that Microsoft is bad and has mismanaged its core business in a way that results in people losing their jobs, and I don’t need to explain to you that the people who are harmed by these decisions aren’t the people making them. Either you know all this or you are actively resistant to the realization.

No, what I want to talk about this week is something different because as we’re gearing up for TennoCon and as I thought about the sort of media that’s actually a palliative response to this kind of awfulness, yes, I thought about Warframe . It’s a game about saving the galaxy… except it’s not about that because you already start too late for the galaxy to be saved in any meaningful fashion, and there’s an almost terrifying hope and love that flows from that.

You might think that I’m just doing a bit because one of the reveals of 1999 was that the universe of Warframe was never really our universe. But that’s honestly not what I meant. In fact, the reveal that this was never the same as our timeline is actually an important point underscoring the positivity I just mentioned. Because as soon as the game starts up, from the moment you are granted control over your first frame, you are taught one thing: The Origin System is a mess.

The Tenno killed the Orokin, and that was good, but in the intervening time, things haven’t gotten better but worse . The Grineer are a giant, thrashing beast that exists just to make war against people who can barely fight back. The Corpus are an omnicorporation with no purpose, chasing credits as a means to an end. The Infestation rages, it isn’t being controlled, and the closest thing anyone has to a seal on the Infestation is barely held together by a bickering family.

But when you peel back the layers on all of that, you find out that… actually, it’s all much worse than any of that implies.

Sitting beneath everything is the Void, with the Man in the Wall sitting at the heart and very clearly manipulating events for some purpose we don’t know. And that the heart of what happened to our player character and informs so many indescribable hells that have been manufactured. The Hex are literally trapped in a time loop that they are aware of only enough to know that it exists and that we can reset it as many times as we want , and the Hex are our allies .

That’s kind of the recurring theme in Warframe . It’s all bad. And once you learn how bad it is, it gets worse. And far more importantly? You can’t fix it.

I mean, sure, you can do something. You’re a Tenno; you are personally more than a match for entire platoons of Grineer. But the thing is that the Grineer have, functionally, infinite platoons. You can stop this one and maybe the next dozen, but you can’t stop all of them. You cannot institutionally wreck the Grineer. The Tenno, as a faction, do not have the numbers or strength to really cause a full-scale revolution that would take out the most material threats to life in the Origin System… and that’s just the basic stuff, never mind the Sentients still roaming.

This is part of why 1999 specifically makes it clear how different the world was even then because it’s part of the point. The Origin System is very screwed up and in need of saving, but the thing is that even if you could somehow reverse time, you would not be faced with a familiar world that you, the player, recognize. Even in 1999, the problems were there. The Techrot had happened, and it would spread, and further problems would arise.

Welcome to Warframe . The world is fucked up, and it is well beyond the point when any of your actions are going to make it not fucked up. It’s all chaotic, everything is broken and messy, and you cannot turn it back into a version of the world you recognize. All of the damage has been done and cannot be undone. The end of the world happened, and then after it was done pulverizing things into dust, it came back around for seconds. You are not going to make this all right.

How can I call this uplifting? Because the game doesn’t end there. The game starts there. It tells you that the world is like this, and then it hands you a gun and asks, “So what are you going to do, Tenno?”

Video games like to tell you to save the world. Warframe does not. You cannot save the world. But you can save something right here. It’s why Duviri’s story ends with the Drifter deciding that saving a life is more important than escape. It’s why Deimos has you pulling a fractured, angry family back together at great cost. It’s why Onkko sits just behind Konzu and Saya, his ex-wife and his best friend, willing to let her think he’s dead and for the two of them to be together because he’d rather have her be happy than do what might make him happiest.

Because sometimes the world is like that. It breaks and you can’t fix it. Something changes beneath your feet, and now you have to find a path forward you never expected to deal with. It sucks, and the temptation is always there to lie down… but the game’s narrative goes the next step.

You can make the world a better place. You can have a tangible impact, even if it’s not a big enough impact to fix the world. You can hammer one corner of the galaxy into a better shape. Even if you can’t pull the Hex out of the loop, you can be a friend to the members trapped, and you can hope that you’ll find a way eventually. Roathe cannot make up for the things he did that he knows were wrong, but he can bring Loid up to reconnect with the family.

items.title: TennoCon 2026!
items.summary: It’s almost time for Warframe’s annual grand celebration! We’re just two weeks away from TennoCon 2026 on July 10-11. Everything begins on July 10 with the return of our Cosplay Contest and special TennoConcert experience at Canada Life Pla
items.content:
It’s almost time for Warframe’s annual grand celebration! We’re just two weeks away from TennoCon 2026 on July 10-11.

Everything begins on July 10 with the return of our Cosplay Contest and special TennoConcert experience at Canada Life Place, complete with a live orchestra conducted by Warframe composer Matthew Chalmers. Then on July 11, we will be broadcasting live from Canada Life Place again for an entire day of panels, community activities and so much more before everything culminates in TennoLive, where secrets that many thought were lost to time will be laid bare — shaking the Origin System to its very core. Prepare yourself accordingly, Tenno.

Watch for Free Rewards

It wouldn’t be TennoCon without plenty of free Rewards on Twitch and Steam that you can earn just by watching! Be sure to link your Warframe and Twitch accounts ahead of time. Starting on July 10, watch the Cosplay Contest from 7 p.m. ET - 8:30 p.m. ET and TennoConcert from 8:30 p.m. ET to 10 p.m. ET for 30 minutes to earn the following, respectively: - Verminia Cosplay Community Display by Community Creator Luckygoblinnn - Singing Lotus Community Glyph (by Community Creator Cyanfacade) + Built Forma Then on July 11, watch any part of TennoCon from 11 a.m. ET - 4:30 p.m. ET and TennoLive from 4:30 p.m. ET to 6 p.m. ET for 30 minutes each to earn the following: - Haalvu (Primary Weapon) - Mesa Prime Please note: those attending the show in-person with a valid ticket will receive Twitch Drop items via promo codes through an email after TennoCon.

Digital Pack

Further enhance your Arsenal with the TennoCon 2026 Digital Pack, featuring the Hunhullus Ephemera, TennoCon2026 Emote and plenty more! https://store.steampowered.com/app/4447660/Warframe_TennoCon_2026_Digital_Pack/ Whether you’re watching from home or joining us in-person, we can’t wait to experience TennoCon 2026 with you all and reveal a bold new future for Warframe!

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Merch
items.summary: As a sequel to one of Warframe's most beloved updates, we felt Jade Shadows: Constellations deserved a showstopper announcement when we revealed it in April of this year. To that end, we enlisted the legendary team at THE LINE Studio to bri
items.content:
As a sequel to one of Warframe's most beloved updates, we felt Jade Shadows: Constellations deserved a showstopper announcement when we revealed it in April of this year. To that end, we enlisted the legendary team at THE LINE Studio to bring you a first-look at Sirius & Orion with a high-octane animated short worthy of the Stalker's enduring legacy. In honor of that incredible artistic collaboration and the dueling rivals at the center of our latest major update, we're pleased to bring you an exclusive, limited run of Warframe x THE LINE // Jade Shadows: Constellations Merch:

Warframe x THE LINE: Sirius T-Shirt

Featuring a stylized design lifted from the animated short, this boxy, relaxed-fit unisex T-shirt imbues its wearer with the focused precision of Sirius. Heavyweight and made from 100% cotton. Color: Dust.

Warframe x THE LINE: Orion T-Shirt

Featuring a stylized design lifted from the animated short, this boxy, relaxed-fit unisex T-Shirt imbues its wearer with the relentless determination of Orion. Heavyweight and made from 100% cotton. Color: Dust.

Warframe x THE LINE: Celestial Clash Poster

Two unstoppable warriors. Face-to-face (and scythe-to-scythe) clashing high above the wastes of the Orb Vallis. Now immortalized in an awe-inspiring, screenprinted 18" x 24" wall poster.

Warframe x THE LINE Merch Collection

Find harmony in the chaos of Sirius & Orion's eternal battle for the future when you bring home all three items from our latest line of merchandise. Includes both boxy, relaxed fit T-shirts honoring the dueling rivals, as well as their showstopping wall poster.

Special Re-release: Stalker Noggle

The Stalker returns once more in his most adorable physical form! Own the Stalker Noggle and add some charming malevolence to your IRL Base of Operations. Made from polyresin. Box size/Dimensions: 12cm x 12cm x 24cm.

BUY NOW

Please note: Quantities are limited. Get yours while they last!

Nidus Collector’s Statue Available for Pre-Order Now!

The Infestation is spreading beyond the Origin System. The ever-evolving Nidus is getting his very own collectible statue, complete with a commemorative coin and exclusive in-game bonuses. Pre-order yours now from the Warframe Store while quantities last!

Nidus Collector’s Statue

Bring the Infestation’s evolution to your collection with this highly detailed Nidus Statue. Standing at approximately 8.5” (21.59 cm) tall and crafted from polyresin, this collectible captures the grotesque power of Nidus in stunning detail. This purchase also includes an exclusive Nidus Coin crafted from Zinc Alloy at 1.75” (4 cm), an in-game Nidus Noggle and 170 Platinum!

BUY NOW

The Infestation waits for no one — secure your Nidus Collector’s Statue and Coin now!

items.title: Warframe dev says 'no one is celebrating' Destiny 2 winding down: 'A game is healthy when you have competitors'
items.content:
When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Bungie) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 11 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Destiny 2 is slowly closing its doors—its servers aren't shutting down, but Bungie has emphasised that it won't be getting any more content, bar a massive quality of life patch that has left it in a relatively good, but inert and unchanging state.

One might, if they were being cynical and cutthroat, think this is cause for celebration—particularly if they were in charge of the competition, as Warframe is. Not so, says community director and live ops lead Megan Everett in a conversation with Eurogamer .

"Truthfully, it's heartbreaking. It's earth-shattering. I never thought I'd ever see the day where I'd read an article from Bungie about Destiny 2 essentially shutting down.

"I'm speaking personally, but I know people would echo it as well. No one is celebrating the fact that this has happened to Destiny, and its players and that story. I know that people have obviously compared us in terms of being 'direct competitors' and stuff like that. But I think a game is healthy when you have competitors."

As someone in the MMO space, it's honestly true— World of Warcraft got its act together (at least, when compared to the catastrophe of Shadowlands ) when a mass exodus of fans drifted to Final Fantasy 14 . Conversely, with FF14's stagnation, it's starting to adopt a lot of the measures WoW and other MMOs did with Evercold, shaking things up .

'There is no world where it makes sense,' Warframe creative director says of Destiny 2's demise: 'It's unthinkable' 'Don't blame yourselves' for Destiny 2's end, community lead tells players: 'No words can accurately depict how lucky we are to have shared these worlds with you' Shock, tears, and relief: How Destiny 2's most popular creators reacted to the end of the legendary shooter My point being, healthy competition tends to drive games to be better than one another—and stop me if this is going off the reservation—but I think we can all broadly agree that we like it when videogames are good.

Everett also says it's a shame that Destiny 2's been shown support much too late: "I think what's unfortunate and sad is they announced this was happening, and suddenly people are showing all this support for them. They've been getting a lot of negativity for all this time, and it's like, where was all this love!

Keep up to date with the most important stories and the best deals, as picked by the PC Gamer team.

"Overall, I hate to see that happen to them. I have close friends who play Destiny over Warframe, and ain't mad about it. We have friends over there, to see this happen to them is devastating to the industry. No one saw Destiny going away"

Harvey's history with games started when he first begged his parents for a World of Warcraft subscription aged 12, though he's since been cursed with Final Fantasy 14-brain and a huge crush on G'raha Tia. He made his start as a freelancer, writing for websites like Techradar, The Escapist, Dicebreaker, The Gamer, Into the Spine\u2014and of course, PC Gamer. He'll sink his teeth into anything that looks interesting, though he has a soft spot for RPGs, soulslikes, roguelikes, deckbuilders, MMOs, and weird indie titles. He also plays a shelf load of TTRPGs in his offline time. Don't ask him what his favourite system is, he has too many. He made his start as a freelancer, writing for websites like Techradar, The Escapist, Dicebreaker, The Gamer, Into the Spine—and of course, PC Gamer. He'll sink his teeth into anything that looks interesting, though he has a soft spot for RPGs, soulslikes, roguelikes, deckbuilders, MMOs, and weird indie titles. He also plays a shelf load of TTRPGs in his offline time. Don't ask him what his favourite system is, he has too many.

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Brings New Packs
items.summary: Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations now available alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once
items.content:
Reinforce your Arsenal with the newest Warframe, Weapons and Customizations now available alongside Jade Shadows: Constellations! Navigate the conflict between clashing rivals and their mentors as you take up the mantle of the Stalker once more. Now you can take these dueling reflections home by purchasing one of our latest Supporter Packs — either with your local currency or on the in-game Market for Platinum!

Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735380/Warframe_Constellations_Sirius__Orion_Bundle/ What might have been becomes reality with Sirius & Orion. Assume the full might of dueling dark futures and follow the stars to constellations of combined power. This bundle includes:
  • Sirius & Orion Warframe*
  • Pride (Scythe Weapon)
  • Wrath (Scythe Weapon)
  • Constellations Fusion Display
  • Twin Bassinet Decoration (Gifting Bonus)
  • 190 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)
Please note: Sirius & Orion function as a single Warframe and not two separate Warframes.

Constellations Gemini Collection

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735400/Warframe_Constellations_Gemini_Collection/ Fight an eternal battle for the future with Vena, the blood-loving fanatic, and Ryoku, the cool and calculating killer. These two bitter rivals are brought together in this collection of Protoframe Gemini Skins. Each skin can switch between Warframe and Protoframe appearances by activating the corresponding Gemini Emote. This bundle includes:
  • Ryoku Gemini Skin
  • Ryoku Gemini Emote
  • Vena Gemini Skin
  • Vena Gemini Emote
  • 375 Platinum (if purchased with real-world currency)

Constellations Complete Collection

https://store.steampowered.com/app/4735390/Warframe_Constellations_Complete_Collection/ Confront the legacy of a doomed future. Is there room for harmony in the present? This bundle includes:
  • Constellations Sirius & Orion Bundle
  • Constellations Gemini Collection

Dante Tytonis Collection

Choose the path of wisdom with the Dante Tytonis Collection. His dark wings harbor secret knowledge and arcane power. This bundle includes:
  • Dante Tytonis Skin
  • Noctua Lophos (Dante's Exalted Tome)
  • Gallistrix Warfan Skin

Razor's Edge Salon Pack

The future might seem doomed, but style doesn’t have to be with this collection featuring Ryoku and Vena’s hairstyles for the Operator and Drifter. -- These bundles are available now via the in-game Market with full regional pricing!

items.title: Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack
items.summary: Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warfra
items.content:
Fitting for a tectonic brawl between cosmic titans, the Jade Shadows: Constellations Soundtrack features radical rock riffs and catalyzing choral cacophonies. Sirius & Orion may be locked in conflict, but you can bask in harmony with Warframe's latest musical tracks, exquisitely performed and produced in-house by our talented Sound Team.

Where can I listen?

You can find the Jade Shadows: Constellations Official Soundtrack on:
  • Spotify
  • Apple Music
  • YouTube Music
  • Amazon Music
  • Tidal
  • Deezer
  • Bandcamp
Additionally, we've created a new playlist with help from UNFD, the record label who helped us bring ERRA's "Crawl Backwards Out of Heaven" to our unforgettable Jade Shadows: Constellations animated short. Find Warframe x UNFD on Spotify and listen to a specially-curated assortment of tracks from Warframe, ERRA and other artists.

FULL TRACKLIST:

1. Celestial Clash
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir:Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
2. Sacred Light
  • Composer: Matthew Chalmers
  • Producers: Erich Preston, Matthew Chalmers
  • Choir: Anaïs Kelsey-Verdecchia, Rayna Crandlemire, Kayla Ruiz, Anika Venkatesh, Sarah Mole, Francesca Hauser, Joshua Sutherland, Nathan Gritter, Tyrese Walters, Kai Leung, Martin Gomes, Henry Paterson
  • Choir Orchestration: Francesca Hauser
  • Choir Engineer: Stephen Koszler
  • Koto: Ray Hickey
  • Shakuhachi: Ron Korb
  • Lyrics: Matthew Chalmers
LYRICS: Where sacred light shines The song of life begins As the shadow of time The protector marches evermore towards silence Glory on high Where the sacred light shines
